The US President put on a weak-sounding voice as he mimicked the Prime Minister saying he couldn't send British forces to the Middle East. My wife found out purely by chance following a slightly elevated LFT in a non connected blood test was then subsequently told the mass in the pancreas was inoperable and that it had also spread to the liver and lungs, she then started Cheamo which can be really hard but she was so strong through it, early results were extremely positive on the kidney and lungs with the mass stable in the pancreas and still had absolutely no symptoms of the disease unfortunately after 15 months she found out that in an extremely rare mutation it had spread to the brain and sadly passed away within a few weeks. Hindsight is a wonderful and also terrible thing and the NHS Oncology team's do a fantastic job but if you're financially able to once diagnosed please go privately for a full body MRI scan to get a full picture of the illness. And one last thing as my wife did stay absolutely positive throughout.

Often dubbed a 'silent killer', pancreatic cancer is one of the deadliest forms of the disease, with just one in four patients surviving beyond a year after diagnosis.

In footage obtained exclusively by the Daily Mail, a black Porsche 911 GT3 RS can be seen parked within a set of yellow lines on a plush side road off Kensington High Street, west London. For years my husband and I supported my father's business efforts worked for next to nothing while he built the family business on the promise of future inheritance. After a 57 year marriage to my dear mum ended with her passing, at 77, he married a 36 year old woman from the Philippines 5 months later. I learned of this from his accountant. When he passed away, she inherited most of his money and took it home. We were left out in the cold because the money was overseas. We did manage to claw some back, but the whole thing destroyed me because I was retired and couldn't earn again. Parents should never hand over their children's inheritance for new partners to plunder and share with their own children. It's just so wrong.

I'm worried that if we don't sort this out now, it could cause serious problems later, especially between our children.

Thousands of NATO soldiers with access to armoured vehicles and heavy weaponry were easily defeated by a handful of Ukrainian drone pilots, the latest episode of the Daily Mail's War on Tape reveals. The military exercise, dubbed Hedgehog, showcased the redundancy of conventional military hardware in the face of unmanned aerial vehicles. The training drill featured 6,000 NATO troops, including Britons, whose goal was to advance and capture a position in the middle of a pine forest with just a few dirt roads running through it.

Taking place just eight months before her tragic death in Paris, the historic trip sparked vast political controversy and saw the princess dubbed a 'loose cannon' by a leading political figure. Conservative government ministers spearheaded a deluge of criticism over the princess's actions and argued that her calls for a worldwide ban appeared to endorse the Labour Party's policy on the issue. Moment 'desperate' man tries to rob bank with a vape disguised as a gun - only to be foiled when staff walk off and alert police

CCTV footage from the incident in January shows Gary Fishlock handing a note to bank clerk at a HSBC branch in Broadstairs, Kent, which read: 'I have a gun, put the money in the bag.' Fishlock then gestured he had a gun in his coat pocket but this was in fact a 'large black vape' which the 64-year-old pushed forward to suggest he was armed and in his other hand he held out a crumpled plastic bag. The baffled staff were able to set off a personal alarm and armed police rushed to the scene where the e-cigarette wielding Fishlock was arrested. Appearing at Canterbury Crown Court yesterday, Fishlock admitted attempted robbery and possession of an imitation firearm with intent to cause fear of violence and was jailed for two years.
  • comments
  • 1 video
  • share Moment man tries to rob bank with a vape disguised as a gun

A Liberal Democrat council has banned a group from putting up England flags, accusing it of using them to 'intimidate' people. Oxfordshire County Council slapped Raise the Colours with a formal legal notice that forbids them from hoisting the St George's cross in the county. Two fakers arguing is hardly news.

Tatiana Elizabeth has accused white influencer Lauren Blake of not being 'honest' after she said AI was to blame for her face appearing on the black model's body in a photo posted to social media this week. The face of Blake, an OnlyFans model, was suspiciously superimposed onto a photo of Elizabeth at the 2024 US Open in New York. It was also incorrectly tagged as being at last week's Miami Open. Although Blake claimed to take 'full responsibility' on Wednesday, she also blamed an artificial intelligence content system and her 'team' for the mishap.

It comes as millions of Christians worldwide are marking the day commemorating the Last Supper of Jesus Christ. In Manila, Mandaluyong city and San Fernando, worshippers walk barefoot through the streets, some carrying heavy wooden crosses with the help of fellow devotees, while others mercilessly flagellate themselves with bamboo sticks and chain-link whips in a re-enactment of Christ's suffering.
